chunk_map.heading: Painting the picture with the LLM [H1, no lede]
Two frontiers: how fast, and the right things

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Referent resolution under a sequential deck read.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'The wizard' and 'infinite chameleon' are introduced and self-explained within chunk 1 itself; 'it'/'that' pronouns in chunks 2-3 resolve to the immediately preceding bullet's subject (context window, your setup).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every backward reference points at a beat that exists.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Opener lecture; no 'the X you did/saw' backward beat references.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Live-demo beats survive a text read.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No guess/predict-before-reveal beat in this file.

chunk 1 "The LLM mirrors your stance": lines 6: "It can do good work and bad work. It will be what fits the session. It flatters you; it calls unfinished work progress. It mirrors your stance." — anaphoric run of "It..." clauses; the closing leg ("It mirrors your stance") restates the chunk header instead of adding new concrete content, the same shape flagged as a known-true finding in the sibling file the-machine-you-just-met.md ("it flatters, it progresses, it mirrors" per slide-sweep.md calibration).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Cut to a pair, or give the closing "It..." leg its own concrete content instead of restating the header.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Internal dialect never lands on a slide.
